topic: A coach has to cut a player from the team
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the locker room felt thick, suffocating. I watched Coach Miller point at Mark, his voice dangerously level, dismantling the argument I’d just been having with him. My jaw tightened so hard I thought my teeth might crack. "This isn't fair," I managed to choke out, my voice sounding thin and reedy even to my own ears. I slammed my fist against the metal bench, the sound echoing sharply in the sudden silence. Every muscle in my body was coiled, vibrating with a furious energy I couldn't seem to contain. When the final decision was delivered, the heat rushed to my face, a flush of pure, burning indignation. I just stared at the floor, refusing to look up, the metallic taste of anger sharp on my tongue.

[story 2]

"You think this is a joke?" I spat the words out, my hands clenched into tight fists at my sides. I paced the length of the room, the worn linoleum floor seeming to mock my agitation. Coach Vance stood by the whiteboard, his expression unyielding, and the sheer injustice of it churned a bitter acid in my gut. I stopped abruptly, planting my feet wide, my chest heaving. "After everything I put in, after the sacrifices—this is how you treat me?" My voice rose, sharp and brittle. I felt a hot pressure building behind my eyes, a desperate need to shout until the walls themselves shook. I shook my head slowly, a tight, jerky movement, refusing to accept the cold finality of the situation.

[story 3]

I couldn't look at him. Every time Coach Davies turned his attention toward me, the tightening in my chest became unbearable. I felt a tremor run through my hands, which I immediately stuffed deep into my pockets, pressing hard enough to bruise my palms. "Unbelievable," I murmured, the word tasting like ash. I took a deep, ragged breath, trying to steady the frantic drumming in my heart. The unfairness of it all was a physical weight, pressing me down. I leaned against the cool cinderblock wall, crossing my arms tightly across my chest, my posture rigid and defiant, a silent declaration that this outcome was utterly unacceptable.
